response.justification: Despite extensive software engineering experience, the candidate lacks demonstrable C++ expertise in industrial automation contexts, with only a brief, non-detailed mention of C++ in a 2014–2015 role; current work is dominated by TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with no evidence of STL, multi-threading, message brokers, IEC 62443, or industrial protocols critical to the role.
strengths: Strong full-stack and cross-platform development experience across web, mobile, and embedded systems.
Proven track record in open-source contributions and technical mentoring through workshops and frameworks.
Demonstrated ability to architect event-driven and distributed systems (e.g., GISCollective, AI-agent infrastructure).
concerns: No recent or substantial C++ work; core requirement of the role is severely underrepresented.
Lacks exposure to industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443), MQTT, REST in automation contexts, or industrial protocols like OPC UA.
No evidence of hands-on experience with on-premises industrial platforms, real-time systems, or secure development lifecycle in manufacturing environments.
